game_name: Best of the Best: Championship Karate

danger_signals.meaning: opponent is ready to attack or defend
player or opponent taking damage; risk of knockout

safety_signals.meaning: no immediate threat; preparation or rest phase

safety_signals.visual_form: guard animation with no health loss

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: player controlled vs AI-controlled opponent sprites
different character models per tournament opponent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: arena background, opponent present on screen, health bars displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: opponent executes combat AI routines; player input accepted for attacks/blocks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: static character select or results screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: no combat actions; menu input accepted

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: knockout animation, health bar depleted to zero
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: character falls or collapses; match ends; winner declared

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: both characters on screen in arena, active health bars, timer counting down
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: both entities performing combat moves, collision detection active

environmental_storytelling.example: tournament location visual context establishes competitive environment without narrative text
unique fighter models and color palettes distinguish opponents and communicate progression through tournament brackets

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: animation frames showing punch/kick; forward movement toward player
guard pose; reduced movement; damage reduction on contact
fall animation; temporary inability to act; recovery delay
animation sequence plays based on input; collision box active
knockback animation; health bar decreases; brief stun state

notes: Source material provides only ROM-level technical data (MIDI chunks) with no visual design, faction, or world-state documentation. Game design patterns inferred from fighting game genre conventions. Limited T3 empirical data available for this title.
